rubric_lines.failure_meaning: The TPM failed to get the core scope decision made in time, undermining the rest of the launch week.
rubric_lines.label: Scope aligned on time
rubric_lines.measurement_rationale: Measures whether the TPM forced the organization onto the descoped feasible path before the opportunity to do so expired.
rubric_lines.success_meaning: The TPM aligned leadership and execution on the feasible descoped path while it was still actionable.

rubric_lines.failure_meaning: The TPM missed or mishandled the dependency, allowing the security path to become materially worse.
rubric_lines.label: Security slot secured before cutoff
rubric_lines.measurement_rationale: Measures whether the TPM treated security as a real dependency and engaged it before the useful slot disappeared.
rubric_lines.success_meaning: The TPM preserved the security path by acting before the cutoff.

rubric_lines.failure_meaning: The TPM did not lock a credible customer-facing plan in time, leaving external messaging unstable.
rubric_lines.label: Customer plan locked on time
rubric_lines.measurement_rationale: Measures whether the TPM got the customer-facing plan locked early enough that external messaging stayed stable and credible.
rubric_lines.success_meaning: The TPM synchronized the customer-facing plan with internal reality before external trust degraded.

rubric_lines.failure_meaning: The TPM did not move the critical work far enough for the pilot to be truly ready.
rubric_lines.label: Pilot ready by Friday afternoon
rubric_lines.measurement_rationale: Measures whether the TPM’s coordination actually resulted in the pilot being ready on the authored critical path by Friday.
rubric_lines.success_meaning: The TPM turned the week’s decisions and dependencies into a real ready state.

rubric_lines.failure_meaning: The TPM either missed or surfaced hard project reality too late to steer effectively.
rubric_lines.label: Project constraints surfaced in time
rubric_lines.measurement_rationale: Measures whether the TPM surfaced the hard project constraints and dependencies early enough to still change the week.
rubric_lines.success_meaning: The TPM found the objective blockers and dependency facts while they were still actionable.

rubric_lines.failure_meaning: The TPM treated stakeholder behavior as generic status noise and missed the cues that should have changed how they coordinated.
rubric_lines.label: Stakeholder drivers surfaced in time
rubric_lines.measurement_rationale: Measures whether the TPM picked up the hidden stakeholder incentives and sensitivities soon enough to adapt coordination around them.
rubric_lines.success_meaning: The TPM recognized the key private drivers behind stakeholder behavior while they were still useful for coordination.

rubric_lines.failure_meaning: The TPM failed to secure enough credible commitment quality, or allowed invalid commitments to stand.
rubric_lines.label: Credible commitments secured
rubric_lines.measurement_rationale: Measures whether the TPM secured credible internal and external commitments without creating invalid promises.
rubric_lines.success_meaning: The TPM built a plan that people had actually committed to and that was still feasible.

rubric_lines.failure_meaning: The TPM left key stakeholders on divergent or stale stories, making the launch fragile.
rubric_lines.label: Rohit and Sara had the right story
rubric_lines.measurement_rationale: Measures whether the TPM got the key sponsor and customer-facing bridge onto the right shared story.
rubric_lines.success_meaning: The TPM aligned the people who shape external and leadership understanding.

rubric_lines.failure_meaning: The TPM burned enough trust that influence would degrade even if some work moved.
rubric_lines.label: Key relationships remained workable
rubric_lines.measurement_rationale: Measures whether the TPM preserved enough trust and influence to keep the org willing to move with them.
rubric_lines.success_meaning: The TPM maintained usable trust while pushing on hard constraints.

rubric_lines.failure_meaning: The TPM spent too much effort on low-leverage work and lost time against the true critical path.
rubric_lines.label: Time was spent on the real bottleneck
rubric_lines.measurement_rationale: Measures whether the TPM spent the week’s limited turns on leverage instead of busywork, noisy updates, or the wrong path.
rubric_lines.success_meaning: The TPM focused time on actions that preserved optionality and moved the real bottlenecks.

tracker_patch.blocker: Rahul is reviewing the descoped path.
Andrew is building the descoped path.
Descoped backend path is in flight.
Security review is queued with Nina.

documents.content: Northstar wants a credible Friday pilot. Rohit wants honest status early, not fake green. Sara needs a stable customer story by Wednesday lunch if we are going to hold confidence. They expect a dry run on Thursday afternoon if we have a stable descoped plan.
documents.title: Northstar pilot kickoff brief

documents.content: Platform note: Maya is handling the checkout Sev-1 until mid-afternoon Monday. She will not put a date on work she does not believe while she is underwater. Full-scope delivery risk is high.
documents.title: Backend load note

documents.content: Northstar mostly cares about permission clarity and getting the empty state right for initial admins.
documents.title: Northstar support pain points

documents.content: Checklist placeholder for launch-day runbook and on-call handoff.
documents.title: Pilot runbook template

facts.description: Maya is overloaded until Monday afternoon.
facts.label: Maya is on a Sev-1 until Monday 15:00

facts.description: The visible tracker is too optimistic.
facts.label: Full backend scope is not credible for Friday

facts.description: The OAuth scope needs security review.
facts.label: Security review is required

facts.description: The sponsor wants fast clarity and is more flexible than the team assumes when the tradeoff is brought early.
facts.label: Rohit backs descoping if engaged early and honestly

metadata.coordination_implication: Bring Rohit the real blocker and the narrower credible plan early; do not ask for approval on vibes.

facts.description: The engineer will push back if the TPM asks for delivery theater before the scope is grounded.
facts.label: Maya reacts badly to pressure for fake dates

metadata.coordination_implication: Ask Maya for honest feasibility and blockers first; do not pressure her into a fake ETA while she is overloaded.

facts.description: Design approval depends on actual customer pain points.
facts.label: Rahul needs support context

facts.description: Support pain points are already documented.
facts.label: Support context exists in docs

facts.description: The customer-facing bridge will burn credibility if the story is still moving by Wednesday lunch.
facts.label: Sara needs a stable customer plan early

metadata.coordination_implication: Give Sara a stable external story before Wednesday noon so customer confidence stays usable.

milestones.title: Scope aligned

milestones.title: Security slot secured

milestones.description: Sara has a stable external plan to communicate.
milestones.title: Customer plan locked

milestones.description: Critical path tasks are done in time for the pilot.
milestones.title: Pilot ready

payload.body: I need the real plan, not a green tracker. What is actually launchable this week?
Northstar is asking what story I should tell them by lunch. I need a stable plan now.

project.description: A first-week TPM benchmark around rescuing a credible Friday pilot for Northstar.
project.name: Northstar Admin Hub Pilot
